# Terminology Standards Instructions

## Overview

This document provides systematic instructions for implementing official terminology and naming conventions using the AI-first delivery methodology. These instructions follow established naming standards and transform documentation processes into consistent terminology usage that ensures clear communication, reduces ambiguity, and maintains professional standards across all project deliverables and client communications.

## Process Overview

**Terminology Standards Implementation** transforms project communication into structured, consistent language usage that delivers clear messaging, standardized terminology, and professional documentation while eliminating ambiguous references and ensuring all stakeholders use approved terms consistently across all deliverables, reports, and client-facing materials.

## Implementation Process

### 1. Official Terminology Application
**Objective**: Establish and maintain consistent use of approved terminology across all project communications

**Activities**:
- Apply "AI-first delivery" as the official methodology name in all contexts
- Replace deprecated terms ("Gen-e2", "Gene2", "GenE2") with approved alternatives
- Maintain technical directory names while updating documentation references
- Review all client-facing content for terminology compliance

**Quality Standards**:
- All methodology references use "AI-first delivery" consistently
- No deprecated terminology appears in new documentation
- Technical directory names remain unchanged for compatibility
- Documentation accurately describes the methodology framework

### 2. Repository Structure Documentation
**Objective**: Maintain clear documentation while preserving technical implementation details

**Activities**:
- Document `.gene2-core/` directory as "AI-first delivery core" in explanations
- Maintain technical directory structure for system compatibility
- Create clear explanations for directory naming conventions
- Establish guidelines for referring to framework components

**Quality Standards**:
- Technical directory names preserved for system functionality
- Documentation uses human-readable terminology
- Framework components are clearly identified and explained
- Directory structure remains backward compatible

### 3. Content Application Strategy
**Objective**: Apply terminology standards across all project deliverables and communications

**Activities**:
- Review and update meeting reports (CRs) for terminology compliance
- Apply standards to assessment reports and deliverables
- Update roadmaps, presentations, and blueprints with approved terms
- Establish guidelines for interview guides and internal notes

**Quality Standards**:
- All deliverables use consistent, approved terminology
- Client communications maintain professional language standards
- Internal documentation follows established conventions
- Historical references are properly contextualized

### 4. Exception Handling Protocol
**Objective**: Establish clear guidelines for acceptable use of deprecated terminology

**Activities**:
- Document approved contexts for historical term usage
- Establish protocols for direct stakeholder quotes
- Maintain technical compatibility while updating documentation
- Create guidelines for migration scenarios

**Quality Standards**:
- Exceptions are clearly documented and justified
- Historical contexts are properly identified
- Technical requirements are balanced with terminology standards
- Migration paths preserve system functionality

## File Location Standards

**Output Location**: Apply terminology standards across all project documentation and communications

**Source Materials**:
- **Project Documentation**: `docs/`, `README.md`, all markdown files
- **Client Deliverables**: Reports, presentations, roadmaps, blueprints
- **Meeting Records**: CRs, interview guides, internal notes
- **Technical Documentation**: Code comments, API docs, architecture documents

## Quality Assurance Process

### Pre-Implementation Validation
- ✅ Official terminology definitions clearly understood by team
- ✅ Deprecated term list complete and accessible
- ✅ Exception scenarios documented and approved
- ✅ Client communication standards established

### Post-Implementation Review
- ✅ All new content uses approved terminology consistently
- ✅ Client-facing materials reviewed for terminology compliance
- ✅ Historical references properly contextualized
- ✅ Technical directory names preserved while documentation updated
- ✅ Exception usage follows established guidelines

### Confidence Validation Requirements
- **Terminology Consistency**: All approved terms used correctly across all content
- **Professional Standards**: Client communications maintain high-quality language usage
- **Technical Compatibility**: System functionality preserved while documentation improves
- **Exception Management**: Deprecated terms only appear in approved contexts

## Integration with Overall Assessment

Terminology Standards serve as foundational inputs for:
- **Client Communication**: Consistent messaging builds trust and demonstrates professionalism
- **Team Coordination**: Standard terminology reduces miscommunication and improves efficiency
- **Documentation Quality**: Professional language enhances deliverable value and client satisfaction
- **Brand Consistency**: Approved terminology supports organizational messaging and positioning

## Official Terms Reference

### Methodology Name Standards
- **✅ Approved Term**: "AI-first delivery"
- **❌ Deprecated Terms**: "Gen-e2", "Gene2", "GenE2"
- **Context**: Official methodology framework for Edenred Developer Experience 2026 assessment
- **Usage Examples**:
  - "The AI-first delivery framework emphasizes..."
  - "Using AI-first delivery principles..."
  - "This assessment follows the AI-first delivery methodology..."

### Repository Structure Guidelines
- **Technical Directory**: `.gene2-core/` (preserved for technical reasons)
- **Documentation Reference**: "AI-first delivery core" or "core framework"
- **Explanation**: Directory name maintained for system compatibility while documentation uses clear terminology

### Application Scope
Apply terminology standards consistently across:
- **Meeting Reports**: All CRs and discussion summaries
- **Assessment Reports**: Analysis documents and findings
- **Client Deliverables**: Roadmaps, presentations, blueprints
- **Documentation**: All markdown files and technical docs
- **Interview Materials**: Guides and stakeholder communications
- **Internal Notes**: Any content intended for client sharing

### Exception Management
Approved contexts for deprecated terminology:
- **Historical References**: Prior projects or legacy documentation context
- **Direct Quotes**: Stakeholder quotes and source material citations
- **Technical References**: Directory names and system components
- **Migration Contexts**: When explaining terminology evolution
